CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Plaintiffs Motion For Entitlement To Attorney Fees And Amount Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Roland, Esq. 415-655-8120 of Burke Williams & Sorensen LLP for Plaintiff.
Robert G. Litts, Esq. 415-937-6222 of Dan Johnson Law Group for Defendant Guneet Bajwa.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues amount of fees. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF FABRICOR LLC, SAMUEL LEIZOREK'S Motion For Entitlement To Attorney Fees And Amount Of Attorney Fees.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs Fabricor LLC and Samuel Leizorek's motion for an order determining their entitlement to attorneys' fees is granted. Defendants Guneet Bajwa and Sushil Patel do not dispute that Fabricor and Leizorek are the parties prevailing on the Guaranty and are entitled under that agreement to an award of "any and all costs, fees and expenses (including without limitation, reasonable attorneys' fees and costs) and expenses incurred by Guaranteed Parties in enforcing any rights and remedies under the Guaranty."

The Court determines that the reasonable amount of attorneys' fees to which Fabricor and Leizorek is entitled is $94,355.00, plus costs in the amount of $5,300.22. Plaintiffs are entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees incurred in bringing and prosecuting the complaint, including seeking writs of attachment (whether or not successful). However, they are not entitled to fees incurred in connection with the cross-complaint (including their demurrer, motion to strike, and an unfiled motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction). Those fees were not incurred "on the contract" or "in enforcing any rights and remedies under the Guaranty," but rather in connection with unrelated claims against third parties and Leizorek on the basis of other agreements, to which Plaintiffs successfully raised a misjoinder defense. Further, the majority of those fees were incurred after the Court issued its February 26, 2019 order granting Plaintiffs' motion for a right to attach order and writs of attachment, which Plaintiffs contend was the precipitating factor that led Defendants to finally make payment on the underlying obligation. The Court has calculated the amount as follows: Plaintiffs seek a total of $315,664.60, of which Defendants have shown that $145,075.00 is attributable to the proceedings relating to the cross-complaint and an additional $50,399.50 is attributable to work unrelated to the guaranty provision and attachment proceedings. (Johnson Decl. 12, 17 & Ex. A.) The Court previously awarded $25,835 in connection with its attachment order, which yields a net award of $94,355.00.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Continued To Jul-17-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANTS VIREO CAPITAL, INC., VENUSTUS HOTEL, LLC, SAMUEL LEIZOREK'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendants Vireo Capital, Inc., Venustus Hotel, LLC and Samuel Leizorke's demurrer to the cross-complaint is sustained without leave to amend based on misjoinder. (See Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(d).) TPC St. Helena is the cross-complainant on the first and second causes of action and seeks to recover on its behalf as well as derivatively on behalf of Spring Mountain Hotel LLC. TPC has no right to maintain the cross-complaint because it is not "[a] party against whom a cause of action has been asserted in a complaint." (Code Civ. Proc. 428.10.) TPC is an interloper. The third cause of action is similarly defective. In that claim, cross-complainants Patel, Bajwa, and TPC allege that Venustus Hotel, LLC breached the parties' buyout agreement. As explained, TPC has no right to maintain that claim. Patel and Bajwa's claim on the buyout agreement fails because it is not a claim against Fabricor LLC or Samuel Leizorek (Code Civ. Proc. 428.10(a)) nor is it a proper claim under Code of Civil Procedure 428.10(b) because it does not arise from the guaranty that is alleged in the complaint.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Litts, Esq. 415-937-6222 of Dan Johnson Law Group for Defendant Guneet Bajwa.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Defendant reports he is not contesting the tentative ruling but contesting the last 2 sentences of the proposed order. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF FABRICOR LLC, SAMUEL LEIZOREK'S Application For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Fabricor LLC and Samuel Leizorek's application for a right to attach order and writ of attachment is granted in the amount of $1,081,315. Plaintiffs have carried their burden to show by a preponderance of the evidence (1) that the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) Plaintiffs have established the probable validity of the claim upon which the attachment is based; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based; and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero. (Code Civ. Proc. 484.090(a); Santa Clara Waste Water Co. v. Allied World National Assurance Co. (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 881, 885 [$2.5 million writs of attachment affirmed where party seeking attachment established the probable validity of its claims]; United Central Bank v. Superior Court (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 212, 215 ["Case law holds that a writ of attachment may issue on a guaranty . . . ."]; Goldstein v. Barak Construction (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 845, 852.) As to the probable validity of Plaintiffs' claim, Defendants do not dispute that they executed the Guaranty by which they personally guaranteed payment to Plaintiffs of $1,055,480; that Plaintiffs made demand for payment of that amount; and that they have failed to pay that debt. Defendants' obligations under the Guaranty were not modified or released by any purported set-off or counterclaim which may be available to PresidioTCG or any other person or entity. Neither any alleged oral agreement nor the parties' subsequent course of conduct could have modified Defendants' obligations under the Guaranty, as by its terms that agreement may be amended only by a writing signed by the party or parties sought to be charged. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

3 Orders are signed. (see scanned orders for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
